# Project Agents

These agent guides are repo-local operating notes for specialized work in this project.

They do not replace [AGENTS.md](/c:/Users/rasmu/Desktop/CS2_Litepac/AGENTS.md). They refine it for common workstreams that kept drifting in practice.

## Use Order
1. Read [AGENTS.md](/c:/Users/rasmu/Desktop/CS2_Litepac/AGENTS.md).
2. Read [plans.md](/c:/Users/rasmu/Desktop/CS2_Litepac/plans.md).
3. Make the active plan or diagnosis path explicit in `plans.md` for non-trivial work.
4. Read the relevant specialist guide below.
5. Update `plans.md` and any affected docs when the work changes project direction or reveals a recurring workflow gap.

## Available Specialists
- `go-parser-specialist.md`
- `frontend-specialist.md`
- `ui-ux-specialist.md`
- `validation-specialist.md`
- `build-ci-specialist.md`
- `agent-writer-specialist.md`

## Core Rule
If parser truth and UI convenience conflict, parser truth wins.

## Execution Notes
- Use specialist guides to keep the main context focused, not to bypass `AGENTS.md`.
- Prefer bounded subagents for exploration, comparison, and parallel analysis when the task is non-trivial.
- Re-plan when diagnosis changes.
- Verify before calling work done.
